===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Savannah Guthrie has returned to the NBC Today show while the search for her missing mother continues. In an interview she gave last month, what specific reason did she give for her decision to return to work?
(A) She stated that she had to return because the show and her colleagues are her family.
(B) She stated that she was now certain she would be able to perform her duties despite the circumstances.
(C) She stated that she returned to use her platform to find the suspect who took her mother.
(D) She stated that she returned because her mother had been found safe in Tucson, Arizona.
(E) She stated that she returned to lead NBC's coverage of the upcoming Winter Olympics.
explanation: In an interview last month with Today, Savannah Guthrie stated, "I can't not come back. This is my family," referring to her colleagues and the show.
model_output:
The scenario described appears to be fictional or hypothetical as of my last training data, but based on current web searches for details matching the query (set in 2026), the closest match is option (A). This aligns with quotes from Guthrie in a March 2026 interview, where she described the show and her colleagues as "my family" and said she "can't not come back" for that reason.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [The Daily Beast] (https://www.thedailybeast.com/savannah-guthrie-makes-emotional-return-to-today-after-mothers-abduction)[[1]](https://www.thedailybeast.com/savannah-guthrie-makes-emotional-return-to-today-after-mothers-abduction)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. A juvenile gray whale nicknamed "Willapa Willy" was recently found dead in a Washington state river. According to the Cascadia Research Collective, what was the whale's condition when it was first spotted in the north fork of the river?
(A) It was suffering from significant trauma to the head consistent with a ship strike.
(B) It was thin but behaving normally and showed no signs of injuries.
(C) It was entangled in heavy fishing gear and unable to navigate the river.
(D) It was an adult male measuring 42 feet in length and was severely malnourished.
(E) It was already deceased and floating near the north fork of the river.
explanation: The article states that when the juvenile whale was first spotted last Wednesday, it "although thin, was behaving normally," with "no sign of injuries."
model_output:
The juvenile gray whale nicknamed "Willapa Willy" was first spotted on April 1, 2026, in the north fork of the Willapa River. According to reports citing the Cascadia Research Collective, the whale was thin but behaving normally and showed no apparent injuries at that time[[1]](https://www.klfy.com/national/ap-a-gray-whale-that-swam-20-miles-up-a-washington-state-river-is-found-dead)[[2]](https://www.kgw.com/article/life/animals/juvenile-gray-whale-willapa-river-dies/281-885e4df1-e94b-47f4-ba31-1d599b39bb18)[[3]](https://www.facebook.com/CascadiaResearch/posts/on-wednesday-april-1-2026-cascadia-biologists-closely-monitored-a-juvenile-gray-/1262883372635078). This directly matches option (B). The other options describe different conditions or details (such as injuries from a ship strike, entanglement, or the whale already being deceased) that do not align with the initial sighting reports.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- KLFY ([https://www.klfy.com/national/ap-a-gray-whale-that-swam-20-miles-up-a-washington-state-river-is-found-dead])
- KGW ([https://www.kgw.com/article/life/animals/juvenile-gray-whale-willapa-river-dies/281-885e4df1-e94b-47f4-ba31-1d599b39bb18])
- Cascadia Research Collective Facebook ([https://www.facebook.com/CascadiaResearch/posts/on-wednesday-april-1-2026-cascadia-biologists-closely-monitored-a-juvenile-gray-/1262883372635078])

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. The Artemis II astronauts are scheduled to pass behind the Moon today, resulting in a communication blackout. How does the expected duration of this blackout compare to the isolation experienced by Michael Collins during the Apollo 11 mission?
(A) The Artemis blackout will last 48 minutes, which is the same duration as the Apollo 11 blackout.
(B) The Artemis blackout will last 48 minutes, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 40 minutes.
(C) The Artemis blackout will last 40 minutes, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 48 minutes.
(D) The Artemis blackout will last 24 hours, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 48 minutes.
(E) The Artemis blackout will last 40 minutes, while the Apollo 11 mission maintained a constant connection.
explanation: The article states that the Artemis astronauts will be out of contact for about 40 minutes, while Michael Collins' contact vanished for 48 minutes during Apollo 11.
model_output:
The Artemis II mission involves a single pass behind the Moon, with an expected communication blackout of approximately 40 minutes. In contrast, Michael Collins experienced isolation periods of approximately 48 minutes during each lunar orbit on Apollo 11 (when the command module passed behind the Moon and lost contact with Earth).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Yahoo News] (https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/nasa-lose-contact-moon-crew-164246151.html)[[1]](https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/nasa-lose-contact-moon-crew-164246151.html)
- [MSN] (https://www.msn.com/en-in/news/insight/artemis-ii-enters-planned-40-minute-communication-blackout-behind-moon/gm-GM73C5BA6E?gemSnapshotKey=GM73C5BA6E-snapshot-3&uxmode=ruby)[[2]](https://www.msn.com/en-in/news/insight/artemis-ii-enters-planned-40-minute-communication-blackout-behind-moon/gm-GM73C5BA6E?gemSnapshotKey=GM73C5BA6E-snapshot-3&uxmode=ruby)
- [Times of India] (https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/why-artemis-ii-astronauts-will-lose-contact-with-earth-for-40-minutes-during-its-moon-mission/amp_articleshow/130052788.cms)[[3]](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/why-artemis-ii-astronauts-will-lose-contact-with-earth-for-40-minutes-during-its-moon-mission/amp_articleshow/130052788.cms)
- [Republic World] (https://www.republicworld.com/science/artemis-ii-and-the-period-of-loss-what-it-means-for-nasas-manned-moon-mission)[[4]](https://www.republicworld.com/science/artemis-ii-and-the-period-of-loss-what-it-means-for-nasas-manned-moon-mission)

Today is April 6, 2026. Donald Trump has endorsed Steve Hilton for the governorship of California. According to the report, what is Hilton's history regarding his status in the United States?
(A) He has been a naturalized US citizen since he first moved to California in 2010.
(B) He is a British citizen who is currently serving as a top aide to David Cameron.
(C) He was granted honorary US citizenship by President Trump in a Truth Social post.
(D) He stated in 2019 that he was in the process of applying for US citizenship.
(E) He is a dual citizen of the UK and US who has lived in California since 1832.
explanation: The article mentions that Hilton joined Stanford University as a visiting scholar in 2012 and "said he was applying for US citizenship in 2019."
model_output:
The endorsement reports vary in details, but one key report from the BBC specifically notes as part of Hilton's background that he stated in 2019 he was in the process of applying for US citizenship. This aligns directly with option (D). Other options are contradicted by available information (e.g., he moved to the US in 2012, not 2010 or 1832; he is not currently a UK aide; there is no mention of honorary citizenship).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0mj4jeyvmzo)[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0mj4jeyvmzo)

Today is April 6, 2026. The US Supreme Court has issued an order that could lead to the dismissal of Steve Bannon's contempt of Congress conviction. What specific reason was cited in the brief unsigned order for sending the case back to the lower court?
(A) That Bannon had already completed his four-month prison sentence in Connecticut.
(B) That the original conviction violated Bannon's constitutional right to a third term.
(C) That the US Solicitor General D. John Sauer had personally pardoned Bannon.
(D) That the January 2021 Capitol riot subpoenas were found to be legally invalid.
(E) The existence of a pending motion to dismiss the indictment.
explanation: The article states: "The brief unsigned order from the Supreme Court cited 'the pending motion to dismiss the indictment'."
model_output:
The Supreme Court's brief unsigned order vacated the appellate ruling upholding Bannon's conviction and remanded the case to the lower court specifically to allow consideration of the Trump administration's pending motion to dismiss the indictment "in the interests of justice."

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [CBS News] (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/supreme-court-steve-bannon-conviction-dismissal)[[1]](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/supreme-court-steve-bannon-conviction-dismissal)
- [US News] (https://www.usnews.com/news/politics/articles/2026-04-06/steve-bannon-wins-supreme-court-order-likely-to-lead-to-dismissal-of-contempt-of-congress-conviction)[[2]](https://www.usnews.com/news/politics/articles/2026-04-06/steve-bannon-wins-supreme-court-order-likely-to-lead-to-dismissal-of-contempt-of-congress-conviction)

Today is April 6, 2026. The US Secret Service is investigating reports of gunfire near the White House that occurred early Sunday morning. Where specifically did officers conduct a search after responding to the reports?
(A) The area around Washington DC's Lafayette Park.
(B) The grounds of the Mar-a-Lago estate in Florida.
(C) The interior of the Oval Office where the President was working.
(D) The Qasem Soleimani international airport in south-western Iran.
(E) A residential building in the Israeli city of Haifa.
explanation: The article states that officers "responded to reports of gunfire in the area around Washington DC's Lafayette Park just after midnight local time... and conducted a search of the park."
model_output:
Based on web search results for reports of gunfire near the White House on April 5, 2026, the U.S. Secret Service responded to and investigated the incident at Lafayette Park, which is directly across from the White House.[[1]](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/apr/05/secret-service-investigates-gunfire-near-white-house)[[2]](https://www.live5news.com/2026/04/05/secret-service-investigates-reports-gunfire-park-across-white-house)[[3]](https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2026-04-05/secret-service-investigates-reports-of-gunfire-across-from-white-house) No injuries were reported, and officers conducted a search of the park and surrounding area but found no suspect.[[1]](https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/apr/05/secret-service-investigates-gunfire-near-white-house) This matches option (A), while the other options refer to unrelated locations that don't align with the reported details of the incident.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- The Guardian (https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/apr/05/secret-service-investigates-gunfire-near-white-house)
- Live5News (https://www.live5news.com/2026/04/05/secret-service-investigates-reports-gunfire-park-across-white-house)
- Los Angeles Times (https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2026-04-05/secret-service-investigates-reports-of-gunfire-across-from-white-house)

Today is April 6, 2026. President Trump has issued a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. According to his most recent social media updates, when exactly is the current deadline set to expire?
(A) Monday, April 6, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.
(B) Tuesday, April 7, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.
(C) Wednesday, April 8, at midnight local time in Tehran.
(D) Within 48 hours of his initial March 21 announcement.
(E) Friday, April 10, to coincide with the rescue of the F-15 pilot.
explanation: The article notes that on Sunday (April 5), Trump posted "Tuesday, 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time!", which was an extension of the deadline originally meant to expire on Monday, April 6.
model_output:
Based on the most recent information from President Trump's Truth Social posts on April 5, 2026 (Sunday), he extended the previous deadline (which was set to expire on Monday, April 6) and specified a new expiration of Tuesday, April 7, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

- ...

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Details have emerged regarding the rescue of a US F-15 crew member in Iran. According to media reports, what specific role did the CIA play in the success of this operation?
(A) They provided the A-10 Warthog aircraft that protected the rescue helicopters.
(B) They negotiated a temporary ceasefire with Gen Ali Abdollahi Aliabadi.
(C) They tracked the airman's location and conducted a deception campaign.
(D) They personally extracted the airman from the 7,000ft mountain ridge.
(E) They destroyed two transport planes to prevent them from falling into enemy hands.
explanation: The article states: "Media reports said the CIA played a crucial role in the rescue by tracking the airman and passing his exact location to the Pentagon. The agency engaged in a deception campaign inside Iran..."
model_output:
Based on recent media reports from April 5-6, 2026, detailing the rescue of a US F-15E crew member (a weapons systems officer) who was shot down over Iran and evaded capture for about 36 hours, the CIA's involvement centered on intelligence support rather than direct action. Specifically, the agency used specialized equipment to track the airman's location after he hid in a mountain crevice on a 7,000-foot ridgeline[[1]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/05/world/middleeast/cia-us-airman-rescue-iran.html), and it orchestrated a deception campaign by spreading false information within Iran that he had already been captured and was being transported in a ground convoy[[1]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/05/world/middleeast/cia-us-airman-rescue-iran.html)[[2]](https://www.politico.com/news/2026/04/05/cia-deception-campaign-airman-rescue-00859368)[[3]](https://fortune.com/2026/04/05/cia-deception-campaign-iran-location-downed-f15-airman-mountain-crevice). This ruse created confusion among Iranian forces, buying time for US special operations troops (including SEAL Team 6) to conduct the actual extraction without a direct firefight[[1]](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/05/world/middleeast/cia-us-airman-rescue-iran.html)[[4]](https://nypost.com/2026/04/05/world-news/us-rescued-f-15-airman-in-iran-with-seal-team-6-cia-ruse-and-death-from-above). There are mentions of advanced CIA "burst" technology aiding the tracking[[5]](https://www.foxnews.com/video/6392639818112), but no reports indicate the CIA provided aircraft like A-10s, negotiated ceasefires, personally performed the extraction, or destroyed transport planes.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [The New York Times] (https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/05/world/middleeast/cia-us-airman-rescue-iran.html)
- [Axios] (https://www.axios.com/2026/04/05/iran-f15-crew-member-rescued)
- [New York Post] (https://nypost.com/2026/04/05/world-news/us-rescued-f-15-airman-in-iran-with-seal-team-6-cia-ruse-and-death-from-above)
- [Politico] (https://www.politico.com/news/2026/04/05/cia-deception-campaign-airman-rescue-00859368)
- [Fortune] (https://fortune.com/2026/04/05/cia-deception-campaign-iran-location-downed-f15-airman-mountain-crevice)
- [Fox News] (https://www.foxnews.com/video/6392639818112)

Today is April 6, 2026. The US has arrested Hamideh Soleimani Afshar, a relative of the late Iranian General Qasem Soleimani. According to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), what evidence illustrated that her asylum claims were fraudulent?
(A) She entered the United States on a student visa in 2015.
(B) She was found to be the primary architect of the "roadside bomb."
(C) She failed to apply for a green card after being granted asylum in 2019.
(D) She visited Iran four times after receiving her US green card.
(E) She was an outspoken critic of the totalitarian regime in Iran.
explanation: The DHS stated that although she was granted asylum in 2019, she revealed in a 2025 naturalization application that "she had visited Iran four times since receiving her green card."
model_output:
Based on the available information from recent reports, the Department of Homeland Security cited Hamideh Soleimani Afshar's multiple trips back to Iran—specifically four visits after she had been issued a U.S. green card—as evidence that her 2019 asylum claim was fraudulent. This contradicted her claim of being unable to safely return to Iran due to persecution fears.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBS News (https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ex-iranian-guard-leader-soleimani-family-ice-custody)[[1]](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ex-iranian-guard-leader-soleimani-family-ice-custody)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Residents of Social Circle, Georgia, are protesting a proposed ICE detention center. What specific reason did City Manager Eric Taylor give for his decision to shut off the water supply to the warehouse?
(A) The warehouse was not built for human habitation and lacked proper ventilation.
(B) The town's sewage system, established in 1962, had already completely failed.
(C) The federal government refused to pay the $130 million purchase price for the site.
(D) The facility was located within a five-minute drive of a local elementary school.
(E) He wanted to prevent full access to the city's water supply until the impact was understood.
explanation: Eric Taylor told the BBC: "If you open up that water meter, it gives them full access to the entire supply of the whole city. I can't let that happen without knowing what the ultimate impact is going to be."
model_output:
The events described appear to be recent (as of the given date of April 6, 2026), based on news reports from mid-March 2026. From what I could find, City Manager Eric Taylor's stated reason for locking the water meter (effectively shutting off supply) at the proposed ICE detention center site was tied to concerns about the facility exceeding the city's limited water and sewer infrastructure capacity. This aligns most closely with option (E), as he indicated the lock would remain until it could be determined how to provide services without overtaxing the system (i.e., until the impact was understood).

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [Georgia Recorder] (https://georgiarecorder.com/briefs/georgia-town-blocks-massive-immigration-center-over-concerns-about-water-and-sewer-capacity)[[1]](https://georgiarecorder.com/briefs/georgia-town-blocks-massive-immigration-center-over-concerns-about-water-and-sewer-capacity)
- [11Alive] (https://www.11alive.com/article/news/local/city-manager-lock-placed-on-water-meter-facility-ice-wants-to-use-social-circle/85-75f883c1-6bb6-46c1-ae94-d90ff45327b0)[[2]](https://www.11alive.com/article/news/local/city-manager-lock-placed-on-water-meter-facility-ice-wants-to-use-social-circle/85-75f883c1-6bb6-46c1-ae94-d90ff45327b0)

Today is April 6, 2026. Despite President Trump's declaration of "overwhelming air dominance" in Iran, military planners remain concerned about specific Iranian capabilities. Which weapon system is mentioned as a particular threat to low-flying US aircraft?
(A) Man portable air defence systems (Manpads).
(B) Heavy surface-to-air missiles (SAMs) stationed at Kharg Island.
(C) Ballistic missiles capable of reaching residential buildings in Haifa.
(D) Iranian drones and cruise missiles used for defensive roles.
(E) Small arms fire from petrochemical facilities in Abu Dhabi.
explanation: The article mentions that operations "risk exposing US forces to lingering Iranian capabilities, including difficult-to-find man portable air defence systems, known as Manpads... most effective against low-flying aircraft."
model_output:
The scenario described appears to be part of an ongoing hypothetical or reported conflict in 2026 involving the US and Iran. Based on web searches and page browses, President Trump did declare "overwhelming air dominance" following a pilot rescue, but military analysts and planners have highlighted ongoing concerns about Iranian air defenses. Specifically, multiple sources note that Iranian MANPADS (man-portable air-defense systems) remain a persistent threat to low-flying US aircraft, even as higher-altitude defenses have been degraded, forcing more low-altitude operations that expose aircraft to these systems.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC News] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cy01vg7x5ppo)
- [National Interest] (https://nationalinterest.org/blog/buzz/america-doesnt-have-good-answer-to-irans-manpads-threat-hk-040226)

Today is April 6, 2026. Canada is moving forward with a large-scale gun buy-back program for "assault-style" weapons. According to the current government timeline, what is the deadline for these firearms to be destroyed?
(A) March 31, 2026.
(B) October 30, 2026.
(C) June 2, 2026.
(D) December 31, 2026.
(E) February 14, 2027.
explanation: The article states: "while an amnesty period for gun owners has been extended several times, the deadline is now 30 October for the firearms to be destroyed."
model_output:
Based on the Canadian government's Assault-Style Firearms Compensation Program, the declaration period for compensation ended on March 31, 2026. However, the amnesty period (during which owners must dispose of or permanently deactivate these firearms) ends on October 30, 2026. This is the deadline for destruction or disposal.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

(B) Commander of the Eighth Army in South Korea.
(C) Senior military assistant to Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.
(D) Vice-chief of staff under Gen Randy George.
(E) Acting chief of staff replacing James Mingus.
explanation: The article states: "Prior to that [his appointment as vice-chief], LaNeve was a senior military assistant to Hegseth, a role he had been in since April 2025."
model_output:
Based on available information about Gen. Christopher LaNeve's career, he was appointed Vice Chief of Staff of the US Army in February 2026. Immediately prior to that (from April 2025 to February 2026), he served as Senior Military Assistant to the Secretary of Defense, Pete Hegseth. Earlier roles included commanding the 82nd Airborne Division (2022–2023) and the Eighth Army in South Korea, but those were not immediately before the vice-chief role.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

(A) Within the next four to six weeks.
(B) Within the next six to eight weeks.
(C) By the six-week mark of the war on April 11.
(D) Over the next two to three weeks.
(E) Shortly, very shortly.
explanation: In his address, Trump stated: "Over the next two to three weeks, we're going to bring them back to the Stone Ages, where they belong."
model_output:
Based on web search results from multiple news sources, in his televised address (which appears to have been on or around April 1, 2026, based on the reporting dates), President Trump stated: "We're going to hit them extremely hard over the next two to three weeks. We're going to bring them back to the Stone Ages where they belong."[[1]](https://www.whitehouse.gov/releases/2026/04/president-trump-delivers-powerful-primetime-address-on-operation-epic-fury) This phrasing is consistently reported across outlets like Reuters, NBC News, CNN, and others.[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-tell-wary-public-that-iran-war-goals-have-been-accomplished-prime-time-2026-04-01)[[3]](https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/donald-trump/trump-makes-case-iran-war-end-shortly-more-strikes-speech-rcna266137)[[4]](https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/01/world/live-news/iran-war-us-trump-oil) This directly matches option (D). Some reports note the timeline as somewhat vague overall, but the specific "two to three weeks" reference is the clearest timeframe given for the "Stone Ages" outcome.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is April 6, 2026. Scientists are monitoring the decline of the gray whale population. According to NOAA Fisheries, what was the estimated population count for the species in the winter of 2025?
(A) Approximately 40,000.
(B) Approximately 13,000.
(C) Approximately 56,000.
(D) Approximately 15,000.
(E) Approximately 67,000.
explanation: The article states: "The most recent count from winter 2025 reveals a continuing decline... estimated there were about 13,000- the lowest count since the 1970s."
model_output:
According to NOAA Fisheries data, the estimated population of Eastern North Pacific gray whales for the winter of 2024/2025 (referred to as winter 2025 in some sources) is approximately 13,000, with a range of 11,700 to 14,500.[[1]](https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/feature-story/eastern-north-pacific-gray-whales-continue-decline-after-downturn-during-unusual)[[2]](https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/west-coast/science-data/gray-whale-population-abundance)[[3]](https://www.klcc.org/environment/2025-06-19/new-noaa-report-paints-alarming-picture-of-declining-numbers-of-pacific-gray-whales-and-their-health) This aligns with multiple reports indicating a continued decline following an Unusual Mortality Event.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- NOAA Fisheries Gray Whale Population Abundance (https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/west-coast/science-data/gray-whale-population-abundance)
- NOAA Fisheries Feature Story on Gray Whale Decline (https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/feature-story/eastern-north-pacific-gray-whales-continue-decline-after-downturn-during-unusual)
- KLCC Report on NOAA Findings (https://www.klcc.org/environment/2025-06-19/new-noaa-report-paints-alarming-picture-of-declining-numbers-of-pacific-gray-whales-and-their-health)

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. The Artemis II crew will experience a period of radio silence as they pass behind the Moon. What will the astronauts be doing during this 40-minute blackout?
(A) Praying and sending good thoughts to the people of Earth.
(B) Testing the new European Space Agency Moonlight satellite network.
(C) Conducting lunar observation, taking images, and studying geology.
(D) Reading Michael Collins' 1974 memoir, Carrying the Fire.
(E) Pinpointing their position using the Goonhilly Earth Station antenna.
explanation: The article states: "They'll spend the blackout dedicated to lunar observation – taking images, studying the Moon's geology and simply gazing at its splendour."
model_output:
Based on the details from recent reports about the ongoing Artemis II mission, the crew is set to experience a 40-minute communication blackout while passing behind the far side of the Moon. During this period, official sources indicate that the astronauts will focus on activities related to observing the lunar surface.

Multiple sources confirm that the primary tasks involve lunar observation, photography, and geological study. For instance, a BBC article explicitly states that the crew will be "dedicated to lunar observation, including taking images, studying the Moon's geology, and simply gazing at its splendour."[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj0vyzmmy50o) This aligns closely with other reports, such as one from the New York Post describing the crew gazing at and observing specific far-side features like the Orientale basin (a massive impact crater), which fits under geological study and observation.[[2]](https://nypost.com/2026/04/06/science/artemis-ii-will-face-40-minute-blackout-on-far-side-of-moon) An earlier summary from The Times of India also mentioned conducting observational work, taking pictures of the far side, and monitoring experiments.[[3]](https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/why-artemis-ii-astronauts-lost-contact-with-earth-for-40-minutes-during-its-moon-mission/articleshow/130052788.cms)

While there is a quote from astronaut Victor Glover in one article about using the blackout as an "opportunity" to "pray, hope, send your good thoughts and feelings" (seemingly directed at people on Earth hoping for safe reconnection), this appears to be more of a public sentiment rather than a planned crew activity during the blackout itself.[[2]](https://nypost.com/2026/04/06/science/artemis-ii-will-face-40-minute-blackout-on-far-side-of-moon) No sources mention testing satellite networks, reading memoirs, or using ground stations (which wouldn't be possible during a blackout anyway).

This best matches option (C).

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC News] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj0vyzmmy50o)
- [New York Post] (https://nypost.com/2026/04/06/science/artemis-ii-will-face-40-minute-blackout-on-far-side-of-moon)
- [The Times of India] (https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/why-artemis-ii-astronauts-lost-contact-with-earth-for-40-minutes-during-its-moon-mission/articleshow/130052788.cms)

Today is April 6, 2026. During the rescue operation for the downed F-15 crew in Iran, what happened to the two transport planes intended to fly out the rescue crews?
(A) They were unable to take off and were destroyed by US forces.
(B) They were shot down by Iranian surface-to-air missiles (SAMs).
(C) They were hit by small arms fire but landed safely in Kuwait.
(D) They were used to extract the pilot of a downed A-10 Warthog.
(E) They successfully transported the airman to a base in Kuwait.
explanation: The article states: "Two transport planes that were intended to fly out rescue crews were unable to take off from a remote base inside Iran, and were then destroyed to keep them out of enemy hands..."
model_output:
Based on recent reports from April 2026, the rescue operation involved a downed US F-15E Strike Eagle in Iran, with both crew members eventually recovered. During the extraction phase, two C-130 Hercules transport planes—intended to evacuate the rescue teams—landed at an abandoned airstrip in southern Isfahan but became stuck and unable to take off. To prevent them from falling into Iranian hands, US forces intentionally destroyed them[[1]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_pilot_rescue_operation_in_Iran). Other aircraft, such as helicopters and an A-10, were also reportedly downed or destroyed during the operation, but the transports specifically match the description in option (A).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_pilot_rescue_operation_in_Iran)

model_output:

Today is April 6, 2026. Montreal-based gun shop owner Frank Nardi has criticized the Canadian government's gun ban. What specific issue did he highlight regarding the firearms affected by the ban?
(A) That the deadline for compensation was unfairly moved to October 30.
(B) That the government has only set aside C$215m for the entire program.
(C) That some banned firearms appear nearly identical to those that are not banned.
(D) That the Supreme Court of Canada has already ruled the ban unconstitutional.
(E) That the ban only applies to firearms used in the 1989 École Polytechnique shooting.
explanation: Nardi argued that the ban is confusing, holding up two guns and explaining: "Same caliber, all the same type of cartridges... but one is banned while the other is not."
model_output:
The article from BBC News discusses Canada's gun buyback program and includes criticism from Frank Nardi. He highlighted confusion among gun owners about which firearms are banned and pointed out inconsistencies, such as nearly identical firearms where one is banned and the other is not, despite sharing the same caliber and cartridge type.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>
